Petition For Order Appointing The San Francisco Public Guardian As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Margie Lee Earles (Settlor/Beneficiary).
Sally Gratz appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Guardian.
Mary Hopkins appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that it has reviewed everything, that Ms. Hopkins petition is not ready for resolution, that it has several procedural issues and asked Ms. Hopkins if she was able to secure a bond. Ms. Hopkins stated that she had issues with getting a bond because she does not have an Attorney, that she did not have the opportunity to pursue another bonding company and that she has not secured a bond. The Court noted that the trust states that the Trustee is to be in California. Ms. Hopkins stated that she is planning to return to California. Ms. Navia stated that her Client has consistently said that she doesn't want anyone else over her finances and that she does not want to discuss this any further. Ms. Gratz provided history/the reasons for inception of the conservatorship, stated that the house was in the trust, that the house was in foreclosure, that the foreclosure happened and that there was an overage of funds that were due to Ms. Earles. Ms. Gratz also stated that the foreclosure funds were collected and secured, that they were used to pay for Ms. Earles' care and that Ms. Earles managed her SSA benefits on her own. In addition, Ms. Gratz stated that they removed the conservatorship because it was their understanding that the family would get involved, that APS got involved, that they then went forward with the conservatorship for placement and that they moved funds from the trust to the conservatorship so that they could get placement for Ms. Earles. Furthermore, Ms. Gratz spoke of the issue of spending down for the purpose of Ms. Earles eventually qualifying for Medi-Cal, that at this point the trust has no money in it, and that because the trust is not funded, they could terminate it, close it and finalize it. Ms. Gratz went on to say that they found placement for Ms. Earles in Crown Bay in Alameda County, that the conservatorship is set up, that there is the possibility that Ms. Hopkins could petition to be the Conservator and that putting Ms. Hopkins in as the Successor Trustee would be for terminating the trust. The Court asked how Ms. Earles is doing at her current placement. Ms. Navia stated that she feels Ms. Earles has a good relationship with the Workers, that Ms. Earles has the Workers running around for her and that she thinks Ms. Earles is happy there. The Court advised Ms. Hopkins that she could petition to be the Conservator and noted that there is nothing in the estate. Ms. Hopkins stated that she understands that there is nothing in the trust. Ms. Gratz offered to provide Ms. Hopkins with information regarding who the Conservator is, stated that the bulk of the accounting will be for the temporary trusteeship. Ms. Navia will request to be discharged in the trust. Status on filing of the first and final account in the trust matter is set for June 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The competing Petitions for Appointment of Successor Trustee are under submission. The Court stated that it is inclined to appoint the Public Guardian as to winding down the trust.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition By Mary A. Hopkins For Permanent Appointment As Alternate And/Or Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Denied

Petition For Order Appointing The San Francisco Public Guardian As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-18-2020 Continued To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sally Gratz appeared by zoom for San Francisco Public Guardian.
Rowena Navia Court Appointed Counsel for Margie Lee Earles appeared by zoom.
Mary Hopkins (Ms. Earles' biological daughter) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that Ms. Hopkins filed a competing petition with a hearing date of December 15, 2020 as well as objections to the Public Guardian's petition. Ms. Hopkins stated that she is the biological daughter of Ms. Earles and that she was adopted by another family. The Court informed Ms. Hopkins that if she were to be appointed as the Successor Trustee she would need to be bondable for the amount of $200,000.00, that she will not be able to be appointed without a bond and that she needs to look into this issue prior to the next hearing to make sure she is bondable in the event she is appointed. Ms. Gratz requested that the Public Guardian be appointed as the Successor Trustee because the office of trustee is vacant and stated that if the Court appoints Ms. Hopkins, then the Public Guardian would be willing to step down. Ms. Gratz also stated that if not, they will need to extend the appointment of the Public Guardian as the temporary trustee because they are holding funds and paying for Ms. Earles' care, and that Ms. Earles has been accepted to Sagebrook and they would like to be able to assist with the transfer. The Court acknowledged Ms. Gratz request and stated that it has not looked at the objections. Ms. Hopkins stated that she does not object to extending the Public Guardian as the temporary trustee. Ms. Navia stated that extending the temporary appointment of the Public Guardian is appropriate. The Petition for Order Appointing the San Francisco Public Guardian as Successor Trustee is continued to December 15, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The temporary trustee is extended to December 15, 2020. Ms. Gratz will e-mail the order to the Court Clerk.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Appointing The San Francisco Public Guardian As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Continued To Aug-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sally Gratz appeared by telephone for San Francisco Public Guardian.
Rowena Navia Court appointed Counsel appeared by telephone for Margie Earles (Conservatee).
Mary Hopkins appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. Gratz stated that they gave notice under Probate Code 17203 to the Beneficiaries of the trust, that the Beneficiaries of the trust are Margie and the conservatorship of Margie, that she thinks there are Grandnieces and that they were noticed on the conservatorship, but not in this. Ms. Gratz also stated that they gave notice under Probate Code 17203(b) to other persons (Mary Hopkins and Adele Williams) whose interest would have been affected by this and that she believes that they provided sufficient notice. In addition, Ms. Gratz stated that Ms. Earles acknowledges Ms. Hopkins as her Daughter, that Ms. Hopkins was adopted, that they don't have any documents regarding the adoption or any documents terminating Ms. Earle as a parent and that she feels Ms. Hopkins may have a case. Ms. Hopkins stated that she would like to be considered as an alternate Successor-Trustee, that she would have been able to take care of things had she known of Ms. Earles' situation and that she is objecting to the appointment of the San Francisco Public Guardian. Ms. Gratz stated that in order to continue to hold and protect the funds and pay for Ms. Earles care, they need to extend the conservatorship of the estate, that otherwise they will have no authority to do so, and that they will be amending the petition for the conservatorship and putting it back on calendar. Ms. Gratz also stated that if Ms. Hopkins is interested in being appointed, she will need to file her own petition. Ms. Hopkins stated that she can have her objections in by the end of July. Ms. Navia is to stay on as Court Appointed Counsel.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.

Petition For Order Appointing The San Francisco Public Guardian As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jun-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Appointing The San Francisco Public Guardian As Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-31-2019 Continued To Apr-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sally Gratz for San Francisco Public Guardian (Temporary Successor Trustee).
Rowena Navia for Margie Earles (Settlor).

Ms. Gratz requested a 90-day continuance stating that they need to do more searching, that there are twelve Siblings who are all deceased, that they need to amend the petition and that they need an extension on the order appointing the Temporary Trustee. Ms. Gratz also stated that the property went into foreclosure and sold in foreclosure, that there was an overage of $200,000.00, that Ms. Earles is unable to handle money and that there was an outstanding amount due for Ms. Earles' overstay on the property. In addition, Ms. Gratz stated that APS found placement for Ms. Earles, that the Temporary Trustee is paying for her placement and her needs, that they now have $180,000.00 and want to hold it, and that this is the least restrictive means other than the alternative of a conservatorship. Ms. Navia stated that her Client wanted to be here, but is not well, that her Client does object to a Successor Trustee, that a continuance however would be a benefit so that her Client can appear and that they may be able to get a Representative Payee. The Petition for Order Appointing The San Francisco Public Guardian as Successor Trustee is continued to April 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court signed an order extending the appointment of the Temporary Successor Trustee.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition For Order Appointing The San Francisco Public Guardian As Temporary Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Granted Per Written Order
